key_insights.insight: The fertile window encompasses the five days leading up to ovulation and the day of ovulation itself due to sperm's ability to survive for up to five days in the female reproductive tract.
key_insights.quote: The fertile window is going to be the five days before and then the day you ovulate...Sperm can live in the female reproductive tract for five days.

key_insights.insight: Having less sex to save up sperm for ovulation is counterproductive; regular intercourse is more beneficial for sperm health and overall chances of conception.
key_insights.quote: One of them we already touched on, which is,oh, you should save up sperm for when you're ovulating...But there's no need to do that. As we know, we want to clear the pipes to keep the sperm coming out healthy and alive.

key_insights.insight: Sperm reach the fallopian tubes within minutes after ejaculation, so practices like elevating the hips or staying in bed are unnecessary.
key_insights.quote: The sperm are inside the fallopian tubes within minutes...So there's no need to prop your hips up on a pillow for 30 minutes or put your feet in the air.

key_insights.insight: Urinating after intercourse can help reduce the risk of urinary tract infections and does not affect the chances of getting pregnant.
key_insights.quote: We know that urinating after intercourse for women decreases the risk of a urinary tract infection.

key_insights.insight: Female orgasm helps in fertility by uterine contraction that helps get the sperm to the eggs faster.
key_insights.quote: We do know that orgasm does help, uterine contractions help get the sperm to the eggs faster.

key_insights.insight: The definition of infertility is trying to get pregnant for a year and not succeeding.
key_insights.quote: Infertility is defined as trying for a year and not getting pregnant within that year.

key_insights.insight: Irregular cycles are a sign that things are not working normally
key_insights.quote: Understanding your cycle tracking for a woman is a reflection of her full health, how's your brain interpreting your entire body. So it is helpful because if you have irregularity, it is a sign that things are not working normally.

actionable_advice.advice: Engage in regular intercourse (every day or every other day) when trying to conceive, instead of saving up for specific ovulation days.
actionable_advice.how_to: Make sex a regular part of your relationship, aiming for intercourse every day or every other day, without focusing solely on ovulation.

actionable_advice.advice: Consult a doctor immediately if you're not having regular periods or are unable to complete active intercourse while trying to conceive.
actionable_advice.how_to: Schedule an appointment with a healthcare professional to address any issues with menstrual cycles or intercourse.

actionable_advice.advice: After intercourse, resume normal activities, including urinating, instead of staying in bed or elevating your hips.
actionable_advice.how_to: After intercourse, feel free to get up, go to the bathroom, and continue your day as usual. Urinating can help prevent UTIs.

memorable_stories: A friend finds trying to conceive makes sex feel like a chore, especially if her partner is away during her fertile window.
contrarian_claims: The idea that saving up sperm by having less sex improves chances of pregnancy is a myth. Regular intercourse is more beneficial.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 187: The Pregnancy Doctor REVEALS 3 Ways To INCREASE Your Chances Of Pregnancy NATURALLY
